description: XSS-Leak via Cross-Origin Redirects (PortSwigger Top 10 / 2025, #8 — Abello). Chrome's network connection-pool exhibits an observable oracle when a cross-origin request triggers a redirect to a same-origin URL. By exhausting the per-host TCP socket pool and timing pool reclaim, attacker infers the redirect target — leaking per-victim state (logged-in user, role, multi-tenant slug, OAuth flow position).

redirect_target_leak_via_pool.description: Sensitive endpoint that redirects to per-user URL (e.g. /me redirects to /u/<username>, /dashboard redirects to /org/<slug>). Attacker site triggers fetch + measures connection-pool stall to learn redirect target.

probes.description: Confirm endpoint emits a 3xx with Location pointing to per-user path. Without Cache-Control: no-store / Vary: Cookie this is XS-leakable via connection-pool timing in Chrome.

variables.note: Verify Location varies with session — if so, XS-leak via pool exhaustion is possible.

probes.description: Probe redirect chain length — attacker exhausts the connection pool to host X, observes whether victim's redirect resolves through pool stall. Multi-hop redirects amplify the timing signal.

oauth_state_via_pool.description: OAuth/OIDC implicit/code flow redirects expose per-user state. XS-leak the redirect_uri target via connection-pool oracle to learn whether victim is logged-in to the IdP or which provider was used.

probes.description: OAuth start endpoint (/authorize, /oauth/login) — measure cross-origin redirect chain length / target hostname via Chrome pool timing. Discloses whether user is already authenticated.
